target: data/sections.json part2b — W001 sign-flip paragraph: steel-man detection threshold defense
notes: All 6 curmudgeon holes from c2 review have already been addressed in the current text. Verified: (1) sigma_distance vs SNR conflation reframed to structural unfalsifiability, (2) W004 noise floor 4.4 nT explicitly cited + W001 noise_floor absence noted, (3) detection thresholds acknowledged as legitimate practice, (4) scoring logic precisely described without override language, (5) direction_correct compute-but-not-count inconsistency documented, (6) precision-to-capability gap quantified. ISS-802 concerns also resolved: causal inference separated from factual claims, scoring criterion cited to ISS-705-verification.json. No further changes needed.
holes_addressed: sigma_distance_vs_snr_conflation — reframed from 7-sigma=detection to structural unfalsifiability

dome_defense_not_preempted — legitimate detection thresholds acknowledged upfront

kernel_of_truth: Detection thresholds are standard magnetometry practice. A 3.73 nT signal in 4+ nT noise genuinely cannot be trusted for sign determination. Acknowledged explicitly in current text.

anticipated_objections.objection: The dome could argue W001 noise floor differs from W004
anticipated_objections.response: Current text addresses this by noting W001 does not report its noise_floor, making independent verification impossible — the gap itself is documented as a transparency failure
